pacing._note: Step-climb pacing. COARSE→FINE→MICRO→HOLD state machine. See pacing.py.
pacing._note_rate_hint: Sender upper speed cap MB/s. 0 = no configured limit.

ram._note: If file fits in available RAM minus reserved, receiver buffers entire file in memory and writes once at end. Eliminates write queue, FINISH overcount, and reduces vCPU pressure.
ram._note_reserved: GB of RAM to keep free for OS and other processes. Supports 2 decimal places.

peer_db._note: Adaptive per-peer pacing memory. Tier is selected by send_count for the remote peer.
peer_db._note_tier0: Tier 0: first-time peers (send_count == 0). Cold start, no loss step-down.
peer_db._note_tier1: Tier 1: send_count 1-9. Start at avg_stable_mbps, gentle step-down on loss.
peer_db._note_tier3: Tier 3: send_count >= 20. Very fine probing for well-characterised links.
peer_db._note_floor: Tiers 1-3 only: loss callback will never drop below start_mbps * loss_floor_ratio.

integrity._note_chunk: Per-chunk xxHash XXH3-64 verification is ALWAYS ON and cannot be disabled by either side.
integrity._note_hash_disabled: SENDER SETTING. true = sender will NEVER compute whole-file SHA-256, regardless of receiver request. hash_disabled overrides everything. Use when SHA overhead is unacceptable.
integrity._note_hash_required: SENDER SETTING. true = sender ALWAYS computes SHA-256 AND the receiver MUST verify it — receiver cannot opt out. false = sender only computes SHA-256 if receiver requests it.
integrity._note_hash_requested: RECEIVER SETTING. true = receiver asks sender to compute SHA-256. This is a REQUEST, not a demand — sender honours it only when hash_disabled is false. false = receiver does not request SHA-256.
